art_highlights: Global Art Market Report 2026: Market sales grew by 4% to $59.6B in 2025; Q1 2026 shows cautious optimism with 51% of experts expecting growth.
Recovery is 'K-shaped': High-end segment (>$10M) and top-tier rarity-driven supply are surging, while middle market continues to face headwinds.
Art Basel Hong Kong 2026 (Mar 27-29) is a key confidence indicator; the Asia Pacific market is showing nuanced recovery.
Generational Shift: Younger, digital-first collectors are driving the market, with 49% of gallery clients being new in 2025.
Channel Shift: Online-only sales decreased by 11% in 2025 as collectors return to in-person transactions and fairs, which now account for 35% of dealer turnover.

market_summary.market_sentiment: Positive momentum building for Q2 2026, though global economic growth remains sluggish.
market_summary.key_drivers: Concentration of wealth in high-end tiers, generational buyer shifts, and a return to in-person art fair dominance.
market_summary.trend: Dissolution of traditional asset categories; market is becoming a fluid 'transvertical' asset class sensitive to liquidity and macro-geopolitics.
